source: user feedback 2026-05-16: /wiki-resume should be enough once per tab/session and should account for session queue state.

loop_start.reason: Compiler loops start from source refs and build handoffs, not prior loop chat memory.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

validation.reason: Fresh validation is preferred; policy may require it for high-risk semantic gates.

next_loop.reason: The next compiler loop should start from the build handoff, not the producing compiler context.
evidence: new session id or recorded context reset

requirements.text: /wiki-resume without explicit task should act as a parallel-work scheduler: read fresh CodeWiki state and session queue scoped leases, then select the next unblocked task whose write scopes do not conflict with active leases.
/wiki-resume TASK-### must honor explicit requested child tasks even when an umbrella task is active, unless the session queue reports a real lease conflict or task policy block.
Injected /wiki-resume prompts should come from skill-owned prompt/template assets and preserve user-supplied follow-up intent instead of discarding it.
Parallel helpers should be able to start with one /wiki-resume invocation per tab/session and receive a narrow task, suggested scoped lease, and conflict-safe implementation prompt.

evidence_mapping.criterion: User found /wiki-resume selected TASK-077 umbrella despite intending TASK-083.
evidence_mapping.evidence: Prompt output showed TASK-077 implementation prompt and discarded following user instructions.

evidence_mapping.criterion: Desired product behavior is seamless parallel work with CodeWiki as source of truth/state.
evidence_mapping.evidence: User stated one /wiki_resume per tab/session should pick next task from fresh state taking session queue into account.

agent_assessment: This requirement belongs across TASK-086 and TASK-083. TASK-086 owns session queue/scoped lease public coordination and should implement /wiki-resume task allocation against fresh queue state. TASK-083 owns skill prompt/template assets and should move /wiki-resume injected prompts into skill-owned templates that preserve user intent. No new task needed; refine existing tasks.
summary: Accepted requirement: /wiki-resume should support seamless parallel work by selecting the next safe task from fresh CodeWiki state and session queue leases instead of defaulting to persisted umbrella focus.

diff_table.current_state: /wiki-resume prefers active/persisted focus, so helper sessions can receive the umbrella TASK-077 prompt instead of a safe child task.
diff_table.desired_state: /wiki-resume without explicit task reads fresh graph/roadmap/session queue state and selects the next unblocked child/atomic task whose scoped lease would not conflict.
diff_table.rationale: Parallel work should be seamless: one /wiki-resume per tab/session should pick safe work using CodeWiki as state source.

diff_table.current_state: /wiki-resume TASK-### can be blocked or overridden by existing active umbrella focus and generated prompt injection discards follow-up text.
diff_table.desired_state: Explicit /wiki-resume TASK-### honors the requested non-closed task unless session queue/task policy reports a real block; injected prompt preserves user-provided follow-up intent.
diff_table.rationale: Helpers need deterministic task selection and must not lose user intent after command invocation.

diff_table.current_state: Resume prompt text is source-owned in src/application/prompt.ts and does not provide conflict-safe scoped-lease suggestions for helper sessions.
diff_table.desired_state: Resume prompt templates live under CodeWiki skill assets and render selected task, suggested lease scopes, conflict explanation, and next command/tool steps.
diff_table.rationale: Prompt/template ownership belongs to TASK-083 skill asset boundary; session queue lease language belongs to TASK-086.

accepted_decisions.summary: /wiki-resume without explicit task reads fresh graph/roadmap/session queue state and selects the next unblocked child/atomic task whose scoped lease would not conflict.
Explicit /wiki-resume TASK-### honors the requested non-closed task unless session queue/task policy reports a real block; injected prompt preserves user-provided follow-up intent.
Resume prompt templates live under CodeWiki skill assets and render selected task, suggested lease scopes, conflict explanation, and next command/tool steps.
